images.altText: The space capsule splashing into the ocean with three red and white parachutes attached.

articles.title: It's a long scroll, but you too can now travel where no human has gone before

children.content: The astronauts of NASA's Artemis mission travelled further from Earth than humans have ever gone before — and returned home safely.

image.altText: A graphic with 3D wireframe rocket at centre, behind it the earth and moon with vector flight path

articles.title: The far side revealed: See photos from Artemis II’s lunar fly-by

children.content: A record-breaking voyage, an earthset in silence, and a total solar eclipse — astronauts on the Artemis II mission experienced sights no human has witnessed in half a century.

image.altText: A muted blue Earth with bright white clouds sets behind the cratered lunar surface
astronuats in helicopter.

children.content: The crew on board the Orion has returned to Earth, splashing down in the Pacific Ocean in a perfect '"bullseye". Follow live.

items.title: Artemis II astronauts hoisted to recovery ship after 'perfect bullseye' landing

updates.title: First images of space crew's splashdown
All four crew members have left the Orion
The first crew member has left the spacecraft

items.title: Israel's mass killing has forced Trump to think about Lebanon

children.content: For weeks, Israel has been intensifying its deadly strikes across Lebanon, and Donald Trump has had little to say. But now he has been forced to make it a priority.

image.altText: Donald Trump looking up in an inquisitive manner

items.title: A look at the sea mines Iran claims are in the Strait of Hormuz

children.content: Iran claims to have littered a central shipping passage in the Strait of Hormuz with sea mines, in a move analysts say could be a "coercive threat" to maintain its grip on the crucial waterway.

image.altText: Two large vessels sit by a dock in the background as two men sit by the water in the foreground.

items.title: Thai fishing port highlights 'big trouble' caused by Middle East war

children.content: Tens of thousands of small and commercial fishing vessels in Thailand are stranded on shore, no longer able to afford diesel, which has more than doubled in price since the Iran war began.

articles.title: 'It's a cutthroat business': Where Australia is sourcing oil right now

children.content: Australia is stitching together a steady supply of fuel through a patchwork of relationships. But how long can that last?

image.altText: Graphic of Australia with shipping routes and a oil tanker vessel sailing in the sea.

articles.title: Iran's grip on Strait of Hormuz 'not part of our planning', Israel ambassador says

children.content: Israeli ambassador Hillel Newman admits his country was surprised by the scope of Iran's response to the Middle East war and that it did not expect Gulf states to be targeted in retaliation strikes.

image.altText: A man talking at a press conference inside Parliament House.

gallery.altText: Two men in bright green shirts look solem as they stand on a fishing boat, cruising through the sea.

thumbnail.altText: Two men in bright green shirts look solem as they stand on a fishing boat, cruising through the sea.

items.title: 'Sounds good to me': How Trump was sold war in Iran

children.content: Extraordinary reporting reveals how Israel made the case for regime change in Iran — and how the fallout is driving a profound shift in American opinion, amid deep uncertainty about what the war has achieved.

image.altText: Donald Trump and Benjamin Netanyahu shake hands.

items.title: Behind Canberra's political veil, the mood is grim

children.content: This is not over. The hard stuff is only just beginning. And it's going to get bumpy, testing the nation's resilience and its political leadership.

image.altText: Anthony Albanese wears a suit and blue tie, and black-rimmed glasses. He stares intently just past the camera.

images.altText: Paul Curtis attempts to palm off Ryan Lester.

children.content: North Melbourne faces its toughest challenge of the season so far against defending premiers Brisbane, while winless Essendon takes on the Demons. Follow live.

items.title: Kangaroos take fight to Lions as Bombers search for first win
Raiders roar to life after dynamic first half

children.content: After four straight losses the Raiders have found some of the football that helped take them to last year's minor premiership. Follow live.

image.altText: A man celebrates after scoring a try in a rugby league match

items.title: High Court has made Bruce Lehrmann's defamation case a complete failure

children.content: The High Court was Bruce Lehrmann's last legal avenue to challenge a Federal Court ruling that, on the balance of probabilities, he raped Brittany Higgins — now that avenue has closed.

image.altText: Bruce Lehrman leaves the Federal Court of Australia in Sydney.

items.title: Lying immobile after a remote chopper crash, Fred felt something biting his leg

children.content: In the aftermath of a 2019 helicopter crash in Kakadu National Park, Fred Hunter lay on the ground covered in blood and fuel, when he felt something biting his leg. On the 20th anniversary of CareFlight, Mr Hunter recounts his dramatic rescue by the service.

gallery.altText: Wide-shot, thigh-up of tan man, white / gray t-shirt, beige shorts, standing among tropical forest trees, looking up at sky

thumbnail.altText: Wide-shot, thigh-up of tan man, white / gray t-shirt, beige shorts, standing among tropical forest trees, looking up at sky

items.title: Police call for information after man found dead in remote NT town

children.content: The Northern Territory Police Force is calling for information from the public after a man was found dead near a roadway in the remote town of Tennant Creek.

image.altText: A Northern Territory police vehicle in Katherine.

items.title: Australians less likely to get bypass surgery now than 20 years ago

children.content: John Ross may be Australia's longest surviving double bypass patient. Here's how has the cardiac operation has evolved over the past 50 years.

image.altText: A black and white photo of a surgical operation.

items.title: Debra Adelaide's best friend died by suicide. She wrote a novel about her

children.content: Debra Adelaide became friends with Gabrielle Carey when they were 12. After Carey died by suicide five decades later, Adelaide tried to address her unanswered questions in a novel.

gallery.altText: A woman with long grey hair and wearing a green long-sleeved top, sitting a garden and patting a dog.

thumbnail.altText: A woman with long grey hair and wearing a green long-sleeved top, sitting a garden and patting a dog.

items.title: This rich, meat-free curry is a budget-friendly way to make a massaman

children.content: Mushrooms are a cost-effective way to make a hearty massaman that is also vego and vegan-friendly.

image.altText: A plate of curry, containing mushrooms and potatoes, sits on rice on a plate. It is topped with chopped green coriander.

items.title: ABC 50/50 quiz: A classic poem, oyster recipes and Itchy and Scratchy

children.content: Every question has only two options: one is right, and the other is wrong. You have an even chance of guessing correctly each time.

image.altText: A cartoon cat and mouse sit on rocking chairs on a porch. Lemonade is on a table between them

items.title: Small oil refinery in outback Queensland could be on cusp of expansion

children.content: The Inland Oil Refinery produces specialised underground mining fuel, heating oils and other speciality solvents, and supplies a small amount of on-road diesel for the local community

gallery.altText: aerial of oil refinery and truck

thumbnail.altText: aerial of oil refinery and truck

items.title: UK charity sues Prince Harry for defamation

children.content: Prince Harry co-founded Sentebale 20 years ago in honour of his late mother, Princess Diana, but now the organisation is suing him for defamation.

image.altText: Prince Harry smiles in front of Sentebale branding, wearing a khaki button-up shirt

items.title: Maila may be just a tropical low by the time it reaches Australia

children.content: After causing damage to islands in the Pacific, Cyclone Maila is downgraded as it heads towards Australia.

image.altText: A satellite image of cyclone Maila off the coast of far north Queensland.

items.title: Milk prices may need to increase to cover soaring fuel and fertiliser costs

children.content: Dairy farmers across Australia are battling crippling fuel and fertiliser costs. The industry is now calling for a 30-cent-per-litre rise in milk prices on supermarket shelves.

image.altText: A line of black and white cows lean their heads into a feeding trough. There is yellow hay in the trough.

items.title: McIlroy's 'just keep swinging' approach takes him to huge Masters lead

children.content: Jason Day does not do a lot wrong in the second round at Augusta National, but Rory McIlroy is looking just about untouchable after two rounds of the Masters.

image.altText: Fans watch Rory McIlroy tee off in the second round of the Masters.

items.title: Teen shuns screens to turn love of chickens into growing egg business

children.content: While many teenagers spend their spare time online gaming or playing sport, 14-year-old Max Lye hangs out with a bunch of chooks and dreams of owning his own egg farm.

gallery.altText: A teenage boy in a red shirt and cap holds two chickens.
A woman in a pink shirt and a boy in a red shirt kneel down with chickens.
A little biy holds a chicken  in his arms.

thumbnail.altText: A teenage boy in a red shirt and cap holds two chickens.
A woman in a pink shirt and a boy in a red shirt kneel down with chickens.
A little biy holds a chicken  in his arms.

items.title: New Zealand cyclone forces cancellation of Supercars race

children.content: A looming cyclone forces organisers to cancel the Supercars race in Taupō on New Zealand's north island.

image.altText: Clouds over the Supercars in Taupō.

items.title: Plans to move residents off Cocos Islands walked back after backlash

children.content: After seething criticism from residents, the Commonwealth has released its final plan to protect the Cocos (Keeling) Islands from rising sea levels.

image.altText: White surf crashes against a seawall of stacked sand bags, with tropical plants and coconut trees in the background.

items.title: UK man jailed for abusing girl may face renewed visa cancellation

children.content: A British child sex offender who avoided deportation is facing a possible visa review, after an ABC investigation.

image.altText: A swing at an Adelaide playground site.

items.title: Town keeps museum supplied with koalas for the freezer

children.content: Most people usually drive past animals killed on the road, but in Armidale, they stop, bag them up, and drop them off at the local museum.

image.altText: A side profile image of a woman wearing glasses face-to-face with a koala.

items.title: Bricks and brutalism being added to heritage list, despite objection of owners

children.content: More modern buildings are being added to South Australia's state heritage list, especially churches, but some owners object to their properties being preserved, worried that it will add cost and complexity to their plans.

gallery.altText: A concrete building with a metal roof

thumbnail.altText: A concrete building with a metal roof

items.title: Shark danger from rotting whale forces ocean swim event to move

children.content: A 40-tonne sperm whale carcass has forced the relocation of Tasmania's biggest open water swim. Organisers of the event had hoped it would be moved, but the state's environment department says that was not feasible.

image.altText: a pink and grey whale carcass in white and aqua waves

items.title: Country church with lone parishioner to close as modern worship evolves

children.content: Anglican priest David Perryman delivers Communion to a congregation of one in a small town about three hours east of Melbourne — but not for much longer.

image.altText: David Perryman

items.title: Fresh push for Murray Basin freight rail to get more produce off trucks

children.content: North-west Victorian farmers are pushing for the completion of the Murray Basin Rail Project, more than seven years after it was originally slated to be finished.

image.altText: old broad gauge rail track near Manangatang in Victoria's west

items.title: Greyhound trainers frustrated as safety issues continue at Queensland track

children.content: Ipswich facility The Q was promised to be "as safe for dogs and trainers as possible", but several dogs died during its trial period and events have been cancelled due to unsafe conditions.

image.altText: a greyhound racing track at sunset

items.title: With most of it not fit to sell, this op shop fixes donated items to reduce landfill

children.content: Every year, lots of new textile items are imported to the Australian market … about 1.55 billion of them. Fast fashion is a leading contributor to overproduction and makes reuse difficult.

image.altText: A woman looking at clothes on a rack in an op shop

items.title: Cost of leaving outback pushes students to train as teachers locally

children.content: Ashlee Toms and Aria Miller graduated from high school and walked back through the school gates as staff less than six months later.

image.altText: Two young teachers stand beside a wall with a sign reading 'Mount Isa Central State School'.

items.title: Kennedy becomes first Australian to break 10-second barrier at home

children.content: Lachlan Kennedy has scorched to a sub-10-second run in the heats of the 100 metres at the Australian Athletics Championships, and promises his best is still to come.

gallery.altText: Sprinter Lachlan Kennedy smiles and points at the camera with two outstretched hands

thumbnail.altText: Sprinter Lachlan Kennedy smiles and points at the camera with two outstretched hands
